title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are considering legislation to regulate AI use in political ads to address concerns about misinformation.
Recent AI attack ads in Massachusetts involve candidates like Marc Lombardo and Brian Shortsleeve using AI against opponents.
Proposed bills H.5093 and H.5094 aim to regulate AI-generated political content by requiring transparency and limiting deceptive media.

title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, from Ashburnham, died after his car fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised over $70,000 to support Teixeira's family with funeral and financial expenses.
The fundraiser aims to provide relief for the family during their grieving period, covering various costs.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz delivered a memorable speech at Fenway Park on April 20, 2013, after the Boston Marathon bombings.
Ortiz's declaration, 'This is our [expletive] city,' resonated with Bostonians and was praised despite the profanity used.
His speech was part of a pregame ceremony honoring those who responded to the bombings earlier in the week.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dominick Tucci, 20, a Holy Cross student, is missing after a canoe accident on Lake Hayward in East Haddam.
One of two men in the capsized canoe was rescued and hospitalized, but later discharged; search for Tucci continues.
Holy Cross is providing counseling services and is in contact with law enforcement during the ongoing search.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a Dorchester youth mentor, was fatally shot on Greenwood Street in 2019 without any arrests made.
Price's mother, Carol Price, continues to seek justice, urging anyone with information to help solve his murder.
Boston police maintain the investigation is active and encourage people to provide anonymous tips to aid the inquiry.

title: The basement of this $4.7m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: 1 Haywood Farm in Lynnfield, a popular spot for holiday decor, is listed for $4.695 million.
The basement features a private club vibe with an eight-person bar, screening room, and wine tasting area.
The property includes outdoor entertainment areas with a stone patio, grilling station, and kids' playscape.

title: Patriots reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots plan to release quarterback Joshua Dobbs, who was backup to Drake Maye in 2025, per NFL Network.
Dobbs, 31, joined the Patriots in 2025 and appeared in four games, completing 7 of 10 passes for 65 yards.
The release follows New England's decision to bring back quarterback Tommy DeVito with guaranteed money.

title: Tracy McGrady questions whether Celtics are ‘still on same the page’ following Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tracy McGrady questions Celtics' unity as Jayson Tatum struggles post-injury return.
Carmelo Anthony and Vince Carter suggest alternative strategies to boost Tatum's shooting.
Celtics' three-point shot attempts decline following Tatum's return after 10-month rehab.

title: One New Englander is in this year’s class at Boston’s Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame: Tom Rush
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tom Rush, 85, from Portsmouth, N.H., is among the 2026 inductees for Boston's Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame.
Rush, credited with discovering Joni Mitchell and James Taylor, joins other inductees like Jackson Browne and Neil Young.
Rush will perform at the March 24 induction ceremony, alongside Judy Collins and others, at Boston's Wang Theatre.

title: Jaylen Brown sheds light on 1-on-1 meeting with Jayson Tatum ahead of playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum discussed team dynamics in a 1-on-1 meeting before Tatum's return to the Celtics.
Tatum returned after a 10-month recovery from an Achilles injury, playing eight games before the upcoming playoffs.
The Celtics hold second place in the Eastern Conference and face the Knicks at Madison Square Garden on April 9.
